content.stem: Study this 3×3 grid pattern. Row 1: star increases in size from left to right. Row 2: circle increases in size from left to right. Row 3: triangle, larger triangle, ___. What completes the pattern?
content.explanation: Each row shows the same shape getting bigger and bigger from left to right. Row 1 shows stars increasing in size, Row 2 shows circles increasing in size, so Row 3 should show triangles increasing in size, making the largest triangle correct.

metadata.misconception_targeted: shape cycling and column pattern confusion

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Students may think the pattern cycles through different shapes rather than maintaining shape consistency within rows
Students may try to apply a column-based pattern instead of the row-based size progression
Students may not recognize that the progression should continue to the largest size
